mnemonics: Incidence Matrix: Rows Represent Vertices, Columns Represent Edges (RRV, CRE)
keyPoints: An incidence matrix is a way to represent a graph using a matrix.
The rows of the matrix correspond to the vertices of the graph.
The columns of the matrix correspond to the edges of the graph.
An entry a_ij is 1 if vertex i is incident to edge j, and 0 otherwise.
For a loop, the entry is typically marked as a 2 (or sometimes 1) in the cell corresponding to the vertex and its self-loop.
commonMistakes: Confusing the incidence matrix with the adjacency matrix (which is vertex x vertex).
Forgetting how to represent a self-loop (loop) and incorrectly marking it as a 1 or leaving it as 0.
Mixing up the assignment of rows and columns (rows are vertices, columns are edges).
